message:
mt-wasm: parallelize Q/K/V/O matmul + RoPE per worker

Extends the MoE parallelism to the attention matmul block. Each
worker handles a contiguous T slice; same matmul kernel, just sliced
input/output pointers — no kernel-signature changes needed. The
banded-attention kernel itself stays single-threaded (sliding-window
attention crosses arbitrary T boundaries; an H_q-direction split
would be the natural parallelism axis but needs a kernel
modification, separate work).

Bug caught + fixed during validation: RoPE reads
`cos_row = cos_ptr + t * (head_dim/2)` and the kernel's internal t
loops 0..tCount, so workers with non-zero tStart need their
cos/sin pointer advanced by tStart rows. Otherwise every worker
applies token-0's rotation angles to its slice. mt1 (single
worker, tStart=0) was bit-exact with single-thread which masked
the bug; mt2+ exposed it via 3.45 maxDiff drift.

Numbers (Node, T=32):

  before this commit   after
  single  : 231 ms     231 ms
  mt × 2  : 161 ms     147 ms  (1.57x speedup, was 1.44x)
  mt × 4  : 113 ms      92 ms  (2.52x speedup, was 2.04x)

Browser smoke + webgpu-smoke still produce 2/2 agreed spans.
